summary: BUG FIX VERIFIED: Divided table (mesa dividida) status bug fixed. When ONE account is paid/closed on a divided table, the table now correctly stays 'occupied' or 'divided' instead of incorrectly turning 'free'. Table only becomes 'free' when ALL accounts are paid. Root cause: billing.py was checking for wrong order statuses ['open', 'in_progress', 'divided'] instead of ['active', 'sent']. Fix applied and all 7 backend tests pass. Frontend verified on Desktop (1920x800), Mobile (375x812), and Tablet (768x1024) viewports.

test_cases: test_pay_one_bill_divided_table_stays_occupied - PASS: Table correctly stays 'occupied' after paying ONE bill on divided table
test_pay_all_bills_divided_table_becomes_free - PASS: Table correctly becomes 'free' after paying ALL bills
test_cancel_one_order_divided_table_stays_occupied - PASS: Table correctly stays 'occupied' after cancelling ONE order on divided table
test_cancel_all_orders_divided_table_becomes_free - PASS: Table correctly becomes 'free' after cancelling ALL orders
test_tables_endpoint_returns_status - PASS: Tables endpoint returns status field
test_table_orders_endpoint - PASS: Table orders endpoint works
test_new_account_endpoint - PASS: New account endpoint exists

elements_verified: Table map loads correctly on all viewports
Divided tables show orange color with diagonal stripes
Legend shows all status colors (Libre, Mis mesas, De otros, Dividida, Por Facturar, Reservada)
Multiple accounts (cuentas) display correctly on divided tables
Navigation between tables and order pages works

bug_fix_details.bug_description: When a divided table (mesa dividida) has 2+ accounts and ONE account is paid/closed, the table was incorrectly turning blue (Libre/free) instead of staying as occupied/divided
bug_fix_details.root_cause: billing.py line 631 was checking for order statuses ['open', 'in_progress', 'divided'] but orders actually use statuses ['active', 'sent']. This caused the query to find 0 other active orders and incorrectly free the table.
bug_fix_details.fix_applied: Changed billing.py lines 631 and 637 from ['open', 'in_progress', 'divided'] to ['active', 'sent']

critical_code_review_comments: The original fix in billing.py used incorrect order status values - this was the actual bug
orders.py cancellation paths correctly use {'$nin': ['cancelled', 'closed']} which works for 'active' and 'sent' statuses
Frontend TableMap.js correctly displays divided tables with orange stripes and ÷ badge
context_for_next_testing_agent: Divided table status bug is now fixed. The key fix was in billing.py changing the order status check from ['open', 'in_progress', 'divided'] to ['active', 'sent']. Test file test_divided_table_status.py covers all scenarios: pay one bill (table stays occupied), pay all bills (table becomes free), cancel one order (table stays occupied), cancel all orders (table becomes free).

rca_of_the_issue.problem: Table incorrectly became 'free' after paying ONE bill on a divided table
rca_of_the_issue.root_cause: billing.py was querying for orders with status in ['open', 'in_progress', 'divided'] but actual order statuses are 'active' and 'sent'. This caused the query to return 0 results, making the code think there were no other active orders.
rca_of_the_issue.fix: Changed status check in billing.py from ['open', 'in_progress', 'divided'] to ['active', 'sent']
rca_of_the_issue.verification: All 7 backend tests pass, frontend verified on 3 viewports
